We know that your job is really amorphous and that almost anyone can be a strategist, but don't feel like you have to constantly justify your existence in an agency setting.
don't just make slide, propel great work
You’re not the smartest in the room, we’re a team. Act like it
that the best work arrives when the strategist behaves creatively, and the creative behaves strategically.
We value your work but feel skeptical when feedback feels only critical rather than constructive.
If it’s just an opinion then it’s just another opinion
If they cannot deliver a fresh strategy, we will!
You're really important. Maybe the most important. So work with us and we'll do your thinking proud.
Creatives aren’t dumb
chill with the jargon, it makes y’all look stupid
A lot of times the first and most obvious idea is not the best idea. If multiple people in a room come up with the same concept or creative, it's probably not that interesting.
get off your high horses and talk to people on the street
We already have a creative boss, thanks.
Consider clients & their decision making processes. Change isn't logic.
You’re not the smartest in the room, and that’s ok.
You're not the cleverest in the room
Stop losing people with vague sentences
Creative is more on your side than you could imagine.
Strategy is so interesting, please share your wisdom and expand on concepts and educate us all :)
We’re in this together. It’s not a competition. Let’s get pissed and laugh it off.
charts are awful. brainstorms are useless. there is too much focus on the "inspirational" language in a brief. Be clear.

I think Australian strategists are some of the best on the planet. Would take them over US ones any day, and over most UK ones (as the UK male planners, especially, incorrectly think they're God's gift to advertising... and they ain't).

in the 20 years I've been doing this I've worked with 3 good strategists (Martin Wiegel, Steve Walls, Ed Booty) the rest have been rigid, inflexible, nervous, afraid and would dump you and the work at the blink of a client's eye
Strategist sometimes think advertising is just research and slides and miss the fun of ideas and how they link to life.

The job title "creative strategist" is a joke. Real creative work means to follow your own strategy deep down the rabbithole. To shoot the video, develop the app, design the logo yourself. With all the struggles that you meet along the way. "Creative strategists" usually come up with poorly thought out ideas – and never experience what it's like to try to make something work, that isn't so genious as it appeared in the brainstorming session, when you wrote it on your postit.
Nope.
I love Strat Scraps. It's the highlight of my inbox. Thank you sincerely for doing this work.
Streatgy leads set the tone , but if they start from a place of being a jerk the kindness is often repayed
A great strategy director and creative director partnership is worth its weight in gold. If you're true collaborators, you'll sell more, better work.
neh

Creatives should be better trained in strategy, and strategists should be better trained in creative. In short, if there were no creatives in the room, strategists should be able to come up with a creative campaign, and vice versa.
The colour palette of this survey is horrible
I think you’re a great strat Alex :)
Good survey.

Strategists can not be separated from creative teams. They are part of the creative department. A strategist's job is to find something interesting to say about a product/brand and a creative's job is to find an interesting way to say it. Otherwise, when agencies sell "strategy" or "brand planning" to a client, it looks like a financial cost rather than a creative value.
great strategist are inspiring
I need marketers and advertisers to just calm the fuck down lol
It blows my mind how long ‘strategy’ takes (and costs)
Strategy is the most valuable part of the creative process when done properly with dedication to great creative output
A good strategist is priceless. I know this having worked with good and bad ones. But when you find a good one, it's a real buzz. Proper fulfilment vibes. So, thanks, I guess?
16 questions are 6 too many
Most creatives don't want you to solve the problem or feedback, we just want you to communicate it clearly so we can step away and solve it in the best way.
nothing else

Show me a truly great ad campaign that actually originated with an amazing strategy from a strategist please. Great insights often come from creatives and the strategy is backed into before a pitch and never referred to again (until the next pitch, “just to level-set”). Thanks for this survey!
Nothing, apart from a big thank you. Love the effort to bridge the gap <3

All jokes aside, the onus is on both parties to collaborate and come together to make great work. Silos don’t do anything except store, starting discussions and sharing ideas has always helped creatives feel more tuned into the process, get ideas going faster, and stay plugged into the project longer in my experience. An creatives have to show up willing to step out of just talking design or copy.
Be patient with creativity
Force clarity. Creative is never give time for anything else other than creative.
